verdict.answer_first: A: starts with the likely fault: "Most likely: your access tokens have a short expiry... refresh path isn't actually working"; B opens with framing: "one of two distinct failure shapes."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder: "Most likely" and "A close second"; B surveys: "one of two distinct failure shapes" plus 3 suspects.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use general JWT diagnostics, but neither claims a fact about the user's actual system beyond likelihood.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it adds concrete discriminators: "Log the JWT verification error reason" and "TokenExpiredError vs JsonWebTokenError/invalid signature."
